title: Task module restructuring, hierarchy, Failed variant, and status roll-up
purpose: The task system is currently flat (no parent/child), in-memory only, and missing a Failed status. Multi-agent coordination requires hierarchical tasks where parents decompose work into subtasks, agents claim tasks atomically, and parent status reflects children. This brief restructures task.rs (591 lines, at the limit) into a task/ folder module and implements the full hierarchy with status roll-up, establishing the foundation for DiskTaskStore (NA-004) and extension wiring (NA-005).
task: Convert tools/task.rs into a tools/task/ folder module with types.rs (TaskStatus, TaskEntry, TaskStore trait), memory.rs (InMemoryTaskStore), rollup.rs (status roll-up logic), and tool.rs (TaskTool). Add Failed as a fifth TaskStatus variant. Add parent_task_id and assigned_agent fields to TaskEntry. Extend TaskStore trait with create_subtask, children, ancestors, claim, create_group, list_groups. Implement status roll-up with priority ordering Failed > Blocked > InProgress > Pending > Completed. Extend TaskTool with new action handlers for all new operations.

requirements.title: Task folder module structure
requirements.spec: The task module SHALL be restructured from a single file into a folder module with separate files for types, in-memory store, roll-up logic, and tool implementation.
acceptance: tools/task/ directory exists with mod.rs, types.rs, memory.rs, rollup.rs, tool.rs
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports
tools/task.rs is deleted
tools/mod.rs updated to reference task as folder module
All existing task tests pass (import paths preserved via re-exports)

requirements.title: Failed status variant and extended TaskEntry
requirements.spec: TaskStatus SHALL gain a Failed variant as the highest-priority status. TaskEntry SHALL gain parent_task_id and assigned_agent fields for hierarchical task trees.
acceptance: TaskStatus enum has five variants: Pending, InProgress, Completed, Blocked, Failed
Failed serialises as "failed" (serde rename_all = snake_case)
TaskEntry gains parent_task_id: Option<String>
TaskEntry gains assigned_agent: Option<String>
parse_status handles "failed" input
Test: TaskStatus serde round-trip includes Failed
Test: TaskEntry with parent_task_id and assigned_agent serialises correctly

requirements.title: Extended TaskStore trait with hierarchy operations
requirements.spec: The TaskStore trait SHALL support hierarchical task operations: creating subtasks, querying children and ancestors, atomic claiming, and named task groups.
acceptance: TaskStore::create_subtask(parent_id, entry) creates a task with parent_task_id set
TaskStore::children(parent_id) returns direct child tasks
TaskStore::ancestors(task_id) returns the chain from task to root (inclusive)
TaskStore::claim(task_id, agent_path) atomically sets assigned_agent only if currently None, returns error if already claimed
TaskStore::create_group(slug) creates a named task group
TaskStore::list_groups() returns all group slugs
All methods have default implementations returning NotSupported errors so existing trait impls compile

requirements.title: InMemoryTaskStore with hierarchy support
requirements.spec: InMemoryTaskStore SHALL implement all extended TaskStore operations for testing and ephemeral sessions.
acceptance: create_subtask sets parent_task_id on the new entry
children filters by parent_task_id match
ancestors walks parent_task_id chain to root
claim uses interior mutability (Mutex) for atomic check-and-set
create_group and list_groups track group names in-memory
Test: create parent, create 3 subtasks, verify children returns 3
Test: create 3-level hierarchy, verify ancestors walks to root
Test: claim succeeds once, second claim on same task fails
Test: create_group and list_groups round-trip

requirements.title: Status roll-up logic
requirements.spec: Parent task effective status SHALL be computed from children's statuses with priority ordering: Failed > Blocked > InProgress > Pending > Completed.
acceptance: effective_status(children_statuses) -> TaskStatus function in rollup.rs
Any child Failed -> parent Failed
Any child Blocked (none Failed) -> parent Blocked
Any child InProgress (none Failed/Blocked) -> parent InProgress
Mix of Pending and Completed (no Failed/Blocked/InProgress) -> parent InProgress
All children Pending -> parent Pending
All children Completed -> parent Completed
Empty children list -> returns the parent's own status unchanged
Test: each priority case verified individually
Test: mixed states (one Blocked + one InProgress) -> Blocked

requirements.title: Extended TaskTool with hierarchy and group actions
requirements.spec: TaskTool SHALL expose all new hierarchy and group operations through the tool's action dispatch.
acceptance: action enum gains: create_subtask, children, ancestors, claim, create_group, list_groups
create_subtask requires parent_task_id and description, returns created task with parent link
children requires parent_task_id, returns list of direct children with effective_status applied
ancestors requires task_id, returns ordered chain to root
claim requires task_id, returns updated task or error if already claimed
create_group requires group_slug, returns confirmation
list_groups returns all group slugs
input_schema updated with new actions and parameters
Test: full cycle — create group, create parent, create subtasks, claim, verify roll-up

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement DiskTaskStore — that is NA-004
SHALL NOT modify norn-cli builder.rs or wiring.rs — that is NA-005
SHALL NOT install SharedTaskStore on ToolContext — that is NA-005
SHALL NOT add hardcoded limits on task count, hierarchy depth, or group count (CO1)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass including all existing task tests
Status roll-up priority ordering verified for all 7 cases — verified by tests
Atomic claim prevents double-claiming — verified by test
Hierarchy traversal (children, ancestors) correct at 3+ levels — verified by test
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
